required_algorithm: Resolve runtime root and allowed roots from envelope/policy.
Deny if target path is outside allowed roots.
Deny if target suffix is not in allowed suffixes, default .json.
Deny if target exists and overwrite mode forbids replacement.
Deny if target path or any existing ancestor is a symlink unless policy explicitly allows it.
Serialize payload with json.dumps(sort_keys=True, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), then reparse to prove valid JSON before touching target.
Create destination parent if envelope permits parent creation and parent remains within allowed root.
Open same-directory temp file with exclusive/random name and restrictive permissions.
Read back target, parse JSON, hash bytes, emit decision packet and optional receipt.
On failure after temp creation, best-effort cleanup temp and emit structured failure event.
security_invariants: No file descriptor is opened for an untrusted target before path/root/symlink checks pass.
Temp file is created in the target directory, never a global temp directory.
The final decision packet contains the target hash and byte length or a failure classification.
Failure-learning event is emitted for all deny/error outcomes except caller-declared dry-run validation.
